decisions.rationale: The MMA group overlaps with boxing on shared class names; adding standard UFC/MMA weight values makes those items uniquely identifiable without restructuring.
The problem is the category scope, not a single item; renaming the group to a broader and accurate cryptography family resolves the ambiguity.
Two items in the divide-and-conquer group are hybrids; replacing them with cleaner divide-and-conquer sorts fixes the overlap while preserving the structure.
The overlap is systemic across multiple habitat-based berry groups at easy tier; fixing it cleanly would require broader recategorization, not 1–4 minimal patches.
The geography labels overlap structurally: several seas can belong both to Russia and to Europe/Asia, so a fair fix would require redesigning the category scheme.
The flagged explanation refers to 'Параллелограмм', but that item is not present in the puzzle. The current groups are internally consistent.
The generic 'Герои' label overlaps with other roles; narrowing it to fairy-tale heroines and replacing the one mismatched male character resolves the issue.
The fish group is too broad relative to the 'other' group; narrowing it to commercially familiar fish removes the overlap without changing items.
Only one item is misclassified: 'Блесна' is a lure, not bait in the same sense as the others. Replacing that item is the minimal fix.
Several groups contain multiple dishes that are not uniquely tied to the stated meat source, so a fair repair would require substantial reworking.
Near-duplicate locality wording across groups creates avoidable confusion; replacing one repeated tradition root is sufficient.
The puzzle repeats the bee identity across two groups; replacing the social-insect item with another familiar social insect removes that duplication.
The items are specifically the Great Lakes, so tightening the label makes the category distinct and factual.
The bee duplication is real, but replacing it cleanly in an easy-tier 'raw materials' group without introducing obscure insects would require broader editorial rework.
The sports-club ambiguity can be reduced with minimal disambiguation of the overlapping Russian brands.
